support_contract.current_gate: TinyLlama Q8_0 current gate; exact Llama 3.2 1B/3B and Llama 3 8B Q8_0 rows are supported for exact-row smoke; broader/full support still requires normalized current-head bundles, and the passing 8B broader 50-token, 512-context, compact chat-template-shapes, and lazy-Q8 hot-path reruns are bounded packs/measurements only
support_contract.support_policy: A model, tokenizer, quantization, API feature, or context length is supported only after tests, docs, and real-model evidence exist for that lane.
support_contract.unsupported_policy: Unsupported combinations should return typed errors instead of silently falling back to best-effort behavior.

supported_quantization.notes: decoded into the reference CPU tensor path
decoded into the reference CPU tensor path

TinyLlama remains the current support gate; exact Llama 3.2 1B/3B and Llama 3 8B Q8_0 rows are supported for exact-row smoke only, while broader family support and full-support normalization still require broader context/template coverage, performance, and current-head durable-bundle evidence

planned_quantization.notes: K-quant lane after simpler quant validation

supported_model_families.notes: LLaMA-style decoder path validated on TinyLlama Q8_0 gate

exact Llama 3.2 1B/3B and Llama 3 8B Instruct Q8_0 have row-specific smoke support; the 8B broader 50-token, first 512-context, compact chat-template-shapes, and retained-block lazy-Q8 hot-path passes are bounded pack/measurement evidence only; broader Llama-family/full support still waits on normalized current-head bundles, broader context/template coverage, and production performance evidence

planned_model_families.notes: broader LLaMA-family instruct support after row-specific parity, API, WebUI, memory/perf, and portability evidence
next likely GGUF architecture family to evaluate
tracked as future lanes, not implied support

model_compatibility.evidence: five-prompt TinyLlama Q8_0 parity gate plus current performance artifacts recorded in STATUS.md
model_compatibility.next_step: extend to larger contexts and additional LLaMA-family/quant targets before broadening support claims

model_compatibility.evidence: the exact bartowski Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-Q8_0 GGUF has exact-row load, completion, chat-completion, frontend-smoke evidence, and compact/prompt-pack parity; Camelid supports exact-row smoke for this row only, not broader/full support
model_compatibility.next_step: preserve exact-row smoke support while normalizing current-head backend parity, repeated 50-token output, API/WebUI evidence, memory bounds, and durable bundle evidence on the approved Ubuntu validation lane before any broader/full-support claim

model_compatibility.evidence: the exact tracked Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-Q8_0 GGUF has exact-row load, completion, chat-completion, frontend-smoke, five-prompt API smoke evidence, compact prompt-token/deterministic 1-token/5-token/bounded 50-token parity, and a broader three-prompt 50-token pack; Camelid supports exact-row smoke for this row only, not broader/full support
model_compatibility.next_step: preserve exact-row smoke support while normalizing current-head repeated parity, API/WebUI evidence, memory/performance bounds, and durable bundle evidence on the approved Ubuntu validation lane before any broader/full-support claim

model_compatibility.evidence: the exact tracked Llama 3 8B Instruct Q8_0 GGUF has compact prompt-token/1-token/5-token/50-token parity, a three-prompt 50-token Ubuntu parity run, API/frontend smoke, bounded-memory evidence, one passing bounded 512-context pack, one passing compact chat-template-shapes pack, and retained-block lazy-Q8 hot-path cost probes; exact-row smoke is supported without broad 8B/full-context, arbitrary-template, or performance-portability promotion
model_compatibility.next_step: preserve exact-row smoke support while broadening context/template coverage, refreshing API/WebUI evidence as needed, and closing production performance/RSS portability evidence on the approved Ubuntu validation lane before any broader/full-support claim; treat the passing broader 50-token, 512-context, compact chat-template-shapes, and lazy-Q8 hot-path artifacts as bounded packs/measurements only

model_compatibility.evidence: planned quant tensor fixtures parse descriptors but reject CPU f32 loading until dequant/matmul support exists
model_compatibility.next_step: implement legacy smaller-quant dequant tests before any real-model support claim

model_compatibility.evidence: planned K-quant tensor fixtures parse descriptors but reject CPU f32 loading until dequant/matmul support exists
model_compatibility.next_step: start after simpler Q4_0/Q5_0 support has loader, matmul, and parity evidence

model_compatibility.evidence: tracked as the next likely GGUF family after LLaMA-family breadth stabilizes

api_features.notes: non-streaming and SSE streaming for loaded supported dense GGUF models
loaded-model tokenizer APIs for supported tokenizer families
typed unsupported until implemented and tested
diagnostic logit surfaces exist; full OpenAI-compatible logprobs remain planned

notes: GGUF metadata, tokenizer metadata, tensor loading, Camelid dense config extraction, and tensor binding are available
public completion endpoints can generate small OpenAI-compatible non-streaming responses and SSE token streams from a loaded Camelid-supported dense GGUF model
capability fields are intentionally explicit so the frontend and providers do not infer unsupported model families or quantization formats
